title: Scaffold Next.js app with Bun (root, src/ pattern)
context: Scaffold with Bun using the src/ pattern, e.g. `bun create next-app@latest <target> --ts --tailwind --app --src-dir --import-alias "@/*" --use-bun --yes` (non-interactive). PREFER the repo ROOT: because create-next-app refuses a non-empty directory, scaffold into a temporary empty dir and then move all generated files (including dotfiles) into the repo root, PRESERVING the loop infra (.git/, .claude/, tasks/, AGENT_LOOP.md, run-backlog.sh, scripts/). If a clean root merge is not possible, FALL BACK to scaffolding into web/.
goal: A Next.js app is scaffolded with Bun using the src/ directory pattern, living at the repo root (fallback: web/), and its dev server serves the default Next.js landing page.
acceptance: Record that we are using bun as our preferred package manager and CLI tool of choice in JOURNAL.md
Record the chosen app root — "." (repo root) or "web" — in JOURNAL.md (a durable decision dependent tasks rely on) so paths can be resolved. All checks below run from that app root.
package.json at the app root exists and lists next, react, and react-dom in dependencies.
The src/ directory pattern is in effect: src/app/page.tsx exists under the app root (app code lives under src/, not directly at the app root).
bun.lock (or bun.lockb) exists at the app root and no package-lock.json / yarn.lock / pnpm-lock.yaml is present there — proving Bun, not another manager, was used.
Run `bun install` from the app root — exits 0.
Run `bun run build` from the app root — exits 0 (Next.js production build succeeds).
Start the dev server on port 3000 in the background from the app root (e.g. `bunx --bun next dev -p 3000`); `curl -si http://localhost:3000` returns HTTP 200.

Using Claude browser automation (mcp__claude-in-chrome__ navigate + screenshot), open http://localhost:3000 and confirm the DEFAULT Next.js landing page renders (Next.js branding plus the 'Get started by editing src/app/page.tsx' / 'Deploy now' text). Describe what was observed and capture a screenshot as proof.
Stop the background dev server after verification.
